item.title: Airlines Push European Union to Ease Impact of Iran Conflict
item.description: Europe’s biggest airlines are pushing the European Union to introduce temporary measures to help them through potential jet-fuel shortages and higher costs caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Asia Markets Begin Clawing Back War Losses on Easing Tensions
item.description: Asian markets, among the hardest hit by the Iran war, are starting to recoup those losses, signaling investors are growing more confident that tensions in the Middle East will ease.

item.title: PolyPeptide Studies Strategic Options as Private Equity Circles
item.description: PolyPeptide Group AG and its billionaire shareholder Frederik Paulsen are working on strategic options for the company.

item.title: Danske Bank Error Exposed 20,000 Protected Client Addresses
item.description: Danske Bank A/S last year inadvertently revealed the addresses of about 20,600 of its clients to outsiders.

item.title: Stegra Gets €1.4 Billion From Wallenberg-Led Group to Save Plant
item.description: Ailing Swedish startup Stegra AB has secured funding from the influential Wallenberg family to help the company complete what will be the world’s biggest green-steel plant.

item.title: Digital Realty Plans €2 Billion Data Center Investments in Italy
item.description: Data-center operator Digital Realty Trust Inc. plans to invest €2 billion ($2.3 billion) in Italy over the next five years as part of a Mediterranean-focused build-out in Europe.

item.title: BP Flags Exceptional Oil Trading Gain as Energy Prices Soar
item.description: BP Plc said its oil trading performance was exceptional in the first quarter as the Iran war caused a surge in prices.

item.title: Novo Taps OpenAI to Speed Development of New Obesity Drugs
item.description: Novo Nordisk A/S will integrate OpenAI’s artificial intelligence across the company to accelerate drug development.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures, Pound Steady on Hopes Over Talks
China’s Battery Exports Surge as War Drives Energy-Supply Crunch
item.description: China’s lithium battery exports surged in the first quarter, reinforcing early signs of demand for alternative power sources to counter the global energy-supply crunch arising from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: KKR’s Sheldon Sees Diversification as Key in Volatile Market
item.description: Credit managers should focus on portfolio diversification, according to KKR & Co., as market volatility remains elevated with the ongoing conflict in Iran.

item.title: Asian Firms Rush to Sell Bonds in Busiest Day in Three Months
item.description: Asia’s primary dollar bond market is staging its busiest session in more than three months as borrowers scramble to capitalize on a fragile pause in Middle East hostilities.

item.title: Malaysia Has Fiscal Room to Support Industries Amid Iran War
item.description: Malaysia still has room to deploy fiscal support for industries impacted by the fallout of the Iran war, according to Zafrul Aziz, chair of the Malaysian Investment Development Authority.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi Cautions Akazawa Not to Comment Further on BOJ
item.description: Japan’s trade chief was given a rebuke for making comments related to monetary policy, with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi advising him to steer clear of such topics in the future.

item.title: Why Stocks Are Breaking Free of Oil Crisis Tyranny
item.description: Resilience in the US suggests a strong appetite to carry on with the bull market.

item.title: China’s Yuan Drive Creates an ‘Opening,’ Euroclear Chief Says
item.description: China’s drive to turn the yuan into an international reserve currency is creating an opening and the next step should be matching regulatory requirements with international standards, said Euroclear Chief Executive Officer Valerie Urbain.

item.title: Japan’s 20-Year Bond Sale Sees Strongest Demand Since 2019
item.description: Japan’s 20-year government bond auction drew its strongest demand since 2019 as elevated yields attracted investors despite uncertainty over talks about ending the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Hong Kong Dollar Bond Sales Boom as Issuers Tap Into Haven Flows
item.description: Global borrowers are flocking to issue bonds in Hong Kong dollars as the currency’s status as a haven from the conflict in Iran bolsters demand for the securities.

item.title: Iran Oil Hoard at Sea Shields China’s Refiners From US Blockade
item.description: A hoard of Iranian crude on tankers at sea and robust onshore stockpiles in China will provide a cushion for the nation’s independent refiners should a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz choke off flows.

item.title: China’s Oil and Gas Imports Shrink on Persian Gulf Turmoil
item.description: Chinese crude oil and natural gas imports fell in March, as the supply crunch in the Persian Gulf began to affect shipments.

item.title: China Is Said to Relax Curbs on Some BHP Iron Ore Cargoes
item.description: China’s state-backed iron ore buyer has told several steel mills in the country they are allowed to purchase some BHP Group cargoes, an apparent concession in a months-long commercial dispute.

item.title: Stephen Jen Says China Has Turned Corner, Sees 10% Stock Rise
item.description: Chinese stocks are poised to gain 10% by year-end as Beijing’s supportive policies drive growth and valuations remain discounted, according to London-based asset manager Eurizon SLJ Capital.

item.title: China’s Export Growth Slowed Sharply in First Month of Iran War
item.description: China’s export growth slowed sharply in March from previous months, reflecting growing strains on the world’s No. 2 economy as the war in Iran upends global energy supply.

item.title: Citigroup Strategists Upgrade US Stocks on ‘Defensive Tilt’
item.description: Citigroup Inc. strategists have joined Wall Street peers in turning more constructive on US stocks, saying elevated war uncertainty supports a preference for higher-quality and defensive companies.

item.title: US-Sanctioned Tanker Tests Trump Blockade With Hormuz Transit
item.description: A US-sanctioned tanker linked to China is making its way through the Strait of Hormuz, testing President Donald Trump’s naval blockade.

item.title: Argentina to Reach IMF Staff Agreement as Early as This Week
item.description: Argentina is expected to reach a staff-level agreement with the International Monetary Fund on the second review of its $20 billion program as early as this week, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Amazon Nears Deal for Globalstar in Push to Rival Musk’s Starlink
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. is in advanced talks to acquire satellite operator Globalstar Inc. in a deal that would boost the tech giant’s efforts to build its own satellite operation, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Copper Rises to One-Month High as Iran-Talks Hope Aids Metals
item.description: Copper rose to the highest level in more than a month and other industrial metals were mainly up on optimism the US and Iran will restart peace talks.

item.title: Goldman, Ardian Bought CIC’s $1 Billion US Private Equity Stake
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and Ardian SAS were on the other side of a $1 billion US private equity sale by China’s sovereign wealth fund, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Philippine Economic Managers Oppose Tax Halt on Diesel, Gasoline
item.description: Philippine economic managers rejected a halt in excise taxes on diesel and gasoline, saying a suspension won’t likely give meaningful relief to consumers from oil price swings due to the Iran war.
